NISS Method (Normal-Inverse-Scramble-Switch)

http://youtube.com/watch?v=b230h49TVkk

A (B) = B' A.

Notation

Use U, D, F, B, L, R for white, yellow, green, blue, orange, red faces, instead of up, down, front, back, left, right faces. For beginner, mark the center pieces with these letters.

Define solved state, scr = scramble, sol = solution, psol = partial solution, #(step) = shortest move count of step, (number / number ) = (current move count / cumulative move count), = cancellation with moves. Assume are algs. If , then is called the solution, reverse or inverse of , denoted by . How to reverse: read the letters backwards, switch CW and CCW turns, and double turns are still double, for example, the inverse of R U' F2 B is B F2 U R'. If , then (since ).

Normal scr and inverse scr are called opposite scr.

Steps

Given scramble , Find a solution. Define .

1a) For scr , find a psol ; 1b) For scr , find a psol ;

2a) For scr, find a psol ; 2b) For scr , find a psol ;

3a) For scr , find a psol ; 3b) For scr , find a psol ;

…………………………………………………………

na) For scr , find a psol ; nb) For scr , find a psol ;

…………………………………………………………

If after doing na) step cube is solved, then is a solution of . In fact,

If after doing nb) step cube is solved, then is a solution of . In fact,

Switch scr: solve cube, reverse sol, switch to opposite scr, do scr, do sol

Example Solve

N = R' U' F L' B2 L' U2 L B2 L' U2 L2 D2 B L U L2 D2 U L' D2 L2 R' U' F.

1a) scr = N, #(cross) = 6 is long. Switch to 1b);

2a) scr = (cross)' N, F2L#1 = U' R2 U2 R U' (5/10), F2L#2 = R' F D2 F' D2 (5/15). #(F2L#3) = 5 is long. Switch to 2b);

3a) scr = (cross F2L#3)' N F2L#1 F2L#2, F2L#4 = R F R2 F2 U F U' (7/26). #(OLL) = 11 is long. Switch to 3b);

4a) scr = (cross F2L#3 OLL)' N F2L#1 F2L#2 F2L#4, PLL = U2 L U L' U B2 D' R D B2 (10-1/45). Cube solved.

I = N' = F' U R L2 D2 L U' D2 L2 U' L' B' D2 L2 U2 L B2 L' U2 L B2 L F' U R.

1b) scr = I, cross = F2 L2 F' L' F' (5/5). #(F2L#1) = 7 is long. Switch to 2a);

2b) scr = (F2L#1 F2L#2)' I cross, F2L#3 = R' D R D' (4/19). #(F2L#4) = 8 is long. Switch to 3a);

3b) scr = (F2L#1 F2L#2 F2L #4)' I cross F2L#3, OLL = F' U' R' U R U' R' U R F (10/36). #(PLL) = 10 is long. Switch to 4a).

Final sol = F2L #1 F2L #2 F2L #4 PLL (cross F2L#3 OLL)' = U' R2 U2 R U' R' F D2 F' D2 R F R2 F2 U F U L U L' U B2 D' R D B2 F' R' U' R U R' U' R U F D R' D' R F L F L2 F2 (45).

Step-by-step sol (each time meeting left or right parenthesis, switch to opposite scr):

(F2 L2 F' L' F') // cross (5/5) % scr = I

U' R2 U2 R U' // F2L#1 (5/10) % scr = (cross)' N

R' F D2 F' D2 // F2L#2 (5/15) % scr = (cross)' N F2L#1

(R' D R D') // F2L#3 (4/19) % scr = (F2L#1 F2L#2)' I cross

R F R2 F2 U F U' // F2L#4 (7/26) % scr = (cross F2L#3)' N F2L#1 F2L#2

(F' U' R' U R U' R' U R F) // OLL (10/36) % scr = (F2L#1 F2L#2 F2L #4)' I cross F2L#3

U2 L U L' U B2 D' R D B2 // PLL (10-1/45) % scr = (cross F2L#3 OLL)' N F2L#1 F2L#2 F2L#4

I = solved state, S = scramble, A, B are partial solutions.

If A' S' B = I, then S A B' = I.

用S打乱魔方, 先做步骤A变成某状态 (例如222).

然后还原魔方, 再用A' S'打乱魔方, 这时一定会出现222, 然后用步骤B还原整个魔方.

那么A B'就是原打乱S的一个解法.

左边写正序，右边写逆序，准备做正序的时候就把右边解法逆过来做一遍加上左边的打乱和左边的解法，准备做逆序的时候就把左边解法逆过来做一遍再加上右边的打乱和右边的解法

NISS具有保砖性

一个置换的逆置换不改变置换的不动点

The inverse of a permutation does not change the fixed point of the permutation.

How to prove mathematically that If there is a 112 (or 122) block, then using NISS we can still get a 112 (122)? By now, I only know this fact: The inverse of a permutation does not change the fixed point of the permutation, which naturally induces the fact of 222 block, or F2L-1 case.

Scramble = N0, Inverse = I0

Partial Solution = N1 (I1) N2 (I2)

Skeleton = N2' N1' I0 I1 I2

Scramble = N0, Inverse = I0

Partial Solution = N1 (I1) N2 (I2) N3

Skeleton = I2' I1' N0 N1 N2 N3
